assumptions: A declared intragroup dividend that is recorded as receivable/payable is treated as a recognised intragroup monetary item.
The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements rather than separate financial statements.

approaches.label: Foreign currency hedge of an intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale: IFRS 9 permits the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to be a hedged item in consolidated financial statements if the related FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
conditions: The dividend receivable/payable is a recognised intragroup monetary item
The group entities have different functional currencies so the FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation
The hedging relationship is formally designated and documented and meets the IFRS 9 effectiveness requirements

approaches.label: Ineligible hedged item
approaches.rationale: Under the general IFRS 9 rule, intragroup items are not eligible hedged items in consolidated financial statements unless a specific exception applies.
conditions: The FX effects on the dividend are fully eliminated on consolidation
The item does not meet the intragroup monetary item exception in IFRS 9 6.3.6

approaches.label: Forecast intragroup transaction hedge
approaches.rationale: IFRS 9 allows certain highly probable forecast intragroup transactions to be hedged for foreign currency risk, but that model does not fit a dividend already recognised as receivable.
conditions: This approach applies only before recognition, while the transaction is still highly probable
The transaction must be denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the entity entering into it
The foreign currency risk must affect consolidated profit or loss

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale: IFRIC 16 addresses hedges of the foreign currency risk of a net investment in a foreign operation, not hedges of intragroup dividend receivables.
conditions: The hedged item would need to be an amount of net assets of a foreign operation
The hedge would need to be documented as a net investment hedge under IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16
